- **Feature Flagging:** Component overrides handle brand styling, but if a new backend feature needs to be dark-launched on Lofts before LA, how is that handled? You need a lightweight feature toggle utility in `packages/shared`.
- **Image Cache Multipliers:** If 4 apps request the same Cloudinary URL via Next.js `<Image>`, Vercel will cache and charge you for that image optimization 4 separate times.

**Severe over-engineering in two places:**

**Conceptually excellent, mechanically terrible.**  
Keep the tiers as a *mental model* and *documentation rule* for your teams to understand SEO risk. Discard the technical implementation. Do not use an authoritative `composition-manifest.ts` file. That is "configuration-over-code" pathology. Let the pages be standard React page files composing shared primitives. 

### 8. Barrel file override pattern
**This is the biggest Next.js-specific technical trap in the document.**
Turbopack and Webpack explicitly struggle with deep wildcard re-exports across workspaces when mixing `use client` directives. Furthermore, forcing developers to configure ESLint to prevent direct imports just to maintain an override facade is highly abrasive.
**The Fix:** Use explicit subpath exports (`import { Button } from '@luxurydomains/ui/button'`). If an app needs to override a component, it just shouldn't import the package version. It should simply render its own `<LocalHero />` inside the page.

Since SEO is your primary revenue engine, your platform needs duplicate-content linters running at the CMS save step (hooking into Supabase inserts) verifying that `ll_blog_posts` isn't exceeding a similarity index vs `la_blog_posts`.

- **Feature flags** — critical when a shared package change affects 4 production domains.
- **Cross-domain user identity** — shared `users`/`favorites` tables imply portfolio-level identity, but there is no product policy on whether favorites on LA should appear on Lofts.
- **Performance budgets & bundle governance** — `packages/ui` will grow. You need CI bundle size gates per app.
- **Hot-reload across workspace packages** — Turbopack claims to support this. Test it explicitly before extracting 60 components.
- **Incident response/runbooks** — one bad migration or shared package deploy can take down 4 revenue sites.

### 6. Overcomplexity check
**Yes, several parts are premature over-engineering:**

- The full composition manifest + 3-tier validator for 4 domains is too heavy. A `CONTENT_MATRIX.md` maintained by humans would suffice at this scale.
- The `packages/tooling/manifest-validator` with AST diffing is platform engineering theater right now.
- `packages/seo/` as a shared package is questionable — SEO is your primary differentiator.
- The brand token TS→CSS generation is acceptable (the `satisfies` safety is nice), but could have been simpler.

**Load-bearing and should keep:**
- Separate apps per domain
- Design token system via Tailwind v4 `@theme`
- Barrel override pattern (with caveats)
- Hybrid database approach
- "All apps must build" CI gate

### 7. 3-tier composition system
**Conceptually good, mechanically over-specified.**

The mental model (unique vs configurable vs shared) is useful for thinking about SEO risk. The implementation (JSON manifest driving `layout` + `sections` arrays) turns it into a mini page builder. This adds friction without proportional value.

**Better:** Collapse to two practical tiers — "Domain-owned" (tiers 1+2 merged, normal page files that compose freely) and "Shared" (tier 3). Keep the manifest only for indexed route classes as documentation/governance, not as runtime orchestration.

### 8. Barrel file override pattern
**It will work but has gotchas.**

It is a pragmatic solution to the "how do we override some components per brand" problem with Turbopack/RSC. The ESLint restriction is necessary.

**Known issues:**
- `export *` harms tree-shaking — use explicit named re-exports
- Mixed client/server components in one barrel can make boundaries less obvious (though Next.js does handle it)
- Debugging "which version am I getting?" can be annoying
- Turbopack HMR across workspace packages is sometimes flaky (test this early)

Separate `ui/server` and `ui/client` entrypoints would be a solid improvement.

### 9. Database design
**The hybrid approach is correct.**

Shared tables for inventory (`apartments`, `buildings`, etc.) with `domain_visibility text[]` + GIN is pragmatic. Per-domain tables for editorial content is the right call for SEO isolation.

**Improvements:**
- `text[]` works but consider a proper `apartment_domains` join table if you ever need per-domain metadata (custom title, publish state, rank, etc.).
- The service-role + application-layer filtering story for ISR pages is a real risk. Add integration tests that assert domain A cannot see domain B content.

### 10. Content strategy enforcement
**Structural separation is necessary but nowhere near sufficient.**

Separate `la_blog_posts` vs `ll_blog_posts` prevents accidental query mixing. It does **not** prevent a stressed team from copy-pasting and lightly rewriting.

**You need:**
- Publish-time similarity checking (Jaccard or simple hash comparison on title + lead paragraph)
- Topic ownership registry per domain
- CMS warnings for near-duplicates
- Explicit editorial process documenting differentiation strategy per domain

This is 60% process, 40% tooling. The architecture only solves the 20% technical part.

### 11. Honest overall assessment
**7/10 as architecture direction, 5.5/10 as execution-ready plan.**

**Strengths:**
- Separate apps per domain is non-negotiable and correct
- Turborepo + source packages + design token pipeline is modern and sound
- Hybrid database approach is pragmatic
- "Extract first, refactor later" + "all apps must build" philosophy is correct

**Weaknesses that must be fixed:**
- Over-reliance on structural validation for SEO protection
- Too many packages and too ambitious UI extraction scope too early
- Missing content governance and operational controls
- Composition manifest + validator is over-engineered for current scale

**Specific changes to reach 8.5/10:**
1. Collapse to 2 tiers, narrow the manifest to indexed routes only, defer the rich validator
2. Reduce initial shared packages to `shared` + `design-tokens` + `data`. Extract UI much more conservatively and only *after* both apps are in the monorepo
3. Add content similarity detection and editorial process documentation
4. Add feature flags and cross-domain user policy
5. Test barrel + HMR + RSC behavior with a small spike before broad extraction

The foundation is solid. The current plan tries to build too much platform too soon and puts too much faith in configuration preventing content duplication. Simplify the platform, strengthen the content/ops side, and this becomes excellent.

# Monorepo Templating Architecture — Design Document

**Status:** Approved (v2 — updated 2026-03-14 with reviewer feedback)
**Date:** 2026-03-12 (revised 2026-03-14)
**Supersedes:** `docs/plans/2026-03-11-shared-package-scaffold-plan.md` (npm package approach)
**Parent doc:** `MASTER.md` Section 3 (Monorepo Architecture)

---

## Goal

Design the sharing/templating architecture for the Luxury Domains portfolio (4 domains: LuxuryApartments, Lofts, D3, D4). One codebase, different brands — WordPress-theme model implemented in Turborepo + Next.js + Tailwind v4. Every feature is shared; every brand looks and feels distinct.

## Architecture Summary

- **Monorepo:** Turborepo + pnpm workspaces at `C:\Dev\LuxuryDomains\`
- **Approach:** Hybrid (Approach C) — standard Next.js file routing + composition manifest per app + build-time cross-domain validation
- **Component overrides:** Barrel file re-export pattern + ESLint enforcement + Harry's Forge sub-component composition
- **Brand tokens:** TypeScript `satisfies` → generated CSS `@theme` blocks (Tailwind v4 native)
- **Data layer:** Shared Supabase with `createDomainClient` factory, domain baked in at construction
- **Deployment:** Separate Vercel project per domain, separate Analytics/GSC/PostHog per domain, shared Sentry with domain tags

## Key Decisions

| # | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Features: same everywhere, configured per domain | Maximizes shared-code multiplier; brand differentiation via composition, not feature exclusion |
| 2 | Visual: 3-tier composition (14 unique + 10 configurable + 15 shared) | Indexed pages need differentiation (doorway page risk); non-indexed can be identical |
| 3 | Infrastructure: transparent portfolio | CoStar model. Separate Vercel/Analytics/GSC. Footer cross-links. Don't hide ownership. |
| 4 | Database: hybrid shared + per-domain | Listings/users shared with `domain_visibility`; blog/editorial/leads per-domain |
| 5 | Indexability: structural enforcement | Composition manifest + build-time validator = can't ship identical indexed pages |
| 6 | Component overrides: barrel re-export | Only mechanism that works with Turbopack + RSC. ESLint enforces usage. |
| 7 | Brand tokens: CSS `@theme` from TS source | Tailwind v4 native. JS presets are legacy. TS `satisfies` catches missing tokens at compile time. |
| 8 | Supabase client: created in each app | `@supabase/ssr` requires Next.js `cookies()` — can't live in framework-agnostic shared package |
| 9 | Migrations: single `supabase/` at monorepo root | Append-only, timestamp-ordered. Supabase branching for parallel dev. |
| 10 | Override design: sub-component composition | Harry's Forge pattern. Brands assemble from shared primitives, not rewrite monoliths. |
| 11 | Domain-owned indexed content by default | Editorial, guides, comparisons, market pages stay per-domain. Shared only for non-indexed or proven-stable templates. |
| 12 | Shared primitives before shared pages | A component becomes shared when used in 2+ apps with stable API. Not preemptively extracted. |
| 13 | Stable result types, not raw DB types | packages/data/ exposes query result interfaces. Raw generated types are internal only. Prevents LA-shaped contract drift. |
| 14 | All apps must build (CI gate) | Any shared package change requires `pnpm build:all` to pass for ALL apps before merging. Not developer discipline. |
| 15 | Packages consumed as source | No pre-build step, no dist/ directories. App bundler (Turbopack) compiles packages. Enables instant hot reload. |
| 16 | Backward-compatible migrations only | Add columns/tables. Never drop in same migration. Destructive changes require two-phase deploy. |
| 17 | Composition validator is a guardrail | Three levels: hard fail (identical indexed routes), warning (>80% overlap), human override (with comment). Not an SEO oracle. |

---

## Section 1: Repository Structure

```
C:\Dev\LuxuryDomains\
├── apps/
│   ├── luxury-apartments/
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── app/                    # Next.js file-based routes
│   │   │   ├── components/             # Brand overrides ONLY (files that differ from packages/ui)
│   │   │   ├── content/               # Domain-specific copy, editorial strings
│   │   │   ├── lib/
│   │   │   │   ├── ui.ts             # Override barrel — single file shows all overrides
│   │   │   │   ├── supabase/         # Next.js-specific client creation (server.ts, client.ts)
│   │   │   │   └── data/             # Per-domain table queries (blog, leads, editorial)
│   │   │   └── composition-manifest.ts
│   │   ├── brand.config.ts            # Token values (satisfies BrandTokens)
│   │   ├── next.config.ts
│   │   ├── postcss.config.ts          # Sets base to monorepo root for @source resolution
│   │   ├── .eslintrc.json             # no-restricted-imports: blocks direct @luxurydomains/ui
│   │   └── vercel.json
│   ├── lofts/                         # Same structure
│   ├── d3/
│   └── d4/
│
├── packages/
│   ├── design-tokens/                 # Brand system (THE contract between apps and shared code)
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── schema.ts             # BrandTokens interface (required + optional tiers)
│   │   │   ├── defaults.ts           # Sensible defaults for optional tokens
│   │   │   ├── luxury-apartments.ts  # satisfies BrandTokens
│   │   │   ├── lofts.ts             # satisfies BrandTokens

│   │   ├── generated/                # CSS files auto-built from TS configs
│   │   │   ├── luxury-apartments.css # @theme block with all CSS vars
│   │   │   └── lofts.css
│   │   └── scripts/
│   │       └── generate.ts           # Reads TS → writes CSS (Turborepo build dependency)
│   │
│   ├── ui/                            # Brand-configurable React components
│   │   ├── primitives/               # Button, Input, Badge, Card — read CSS vars, zero brand logic
│   │   ├── compositions/             # Header, Footer, Hero, SearchBar, ListingCard
│   │   │                              # Expose sub-components for brand assembly (Harry's Forge)
│   │   │                              # e.g., Hero exports HeroLayout + HeroImage + HeroHeadline
│   │   └── layouts/                  # PageShell, SplitLayout, GridLayout — structural only
│   │
│   ├── data/                          # Shared Supabase layer (shared tables only)
│   │   ├── client.ts                 # createDomainClient factory (accepts SupabaseClient + domain)
│   │   ├── types.ts                  # Generated from supabase gen types (single source of truth)
│   │   ├── queries/                  # Shared table queries (apartments, buildings, neighborhoods)
│   │   └── filters.ts               # domain_visibility array logic
│   │
│   ├── seo/                           # SEO utilities (primary revenue driver)
│   │   ├── metadata.ts              # generateMetadata() — reads brand config
│   │   ├── json-ld.ts               # Schema generators (Organization, ApartmentComplex, Article)
│   │   ├── sitemap.ts               # Reads composition manifest → generates sitemap entries
│   │   ├── robots.ts                # Domain-aware robots.txt (isProductionDomain pattern)
│   │   └── og.ts                    # OG image generation helpers
│   │
│   ├── shared/                        # Pure utilities (zero React, zero brand, zero Supabase)
│   │   ├── rate-limit.ts
│   │   ├── validation.ts            # Zod schemas for forms
│   │   ├── sanitize.ts
│   │   ├── email.ts                 # Resend abstraction
│   │   ├── calendar.ts
│   │   ├── csv-export.ts
│   │   └── analytics.ts             # PostHog/GA helpers (domain as param, never hardcoded)
│   │
│   ├── tooling/                       # Internal build-time packages
│   │   ├── manifest-validator/       # Cross-domain composition diffing (fails build on doorway risk)
│   │   ├── page-scaffolder/          # Shared logic behind /add-page skill
│   │   └── brand-validator/          # Validates brand.config.ts against schema
│   │
│   └── config/                        # Shared configs
│       ├── typescript/               # Base tsconfig
│       └── eslint/                   # Shared ESLint rules
│
├── supabase/                          # Single migration source (not per-app)
│   ├── migrations/                   # Append-only, timestamp-ordered
│   ├── seed.sql
│   └── config.toml
│
├── turbo.json
├── pnpm-workspace.yaml
└── package.json
```

### Design Rules

1. **File structure resolves brand differences, never conditionals.** `if (brand === 'lofts')` inside a shared component is a code smell. Override via the barrel file instead.
2. **Brand tokens are CSS custom properties.** Components use `bg-primary`, `font-display`, `rounded-card` — Tailwind reads the CSS vars set by the brand's `@theme` block. Zero runtime cost.
3. **Brand schema is a strict TypeScript contract.** Every `brand.config.ts` must `satisfies BrandTokens`. Missing token = build failure.
4. **Data queries accept domain via factory, never as manual parameter.** `createDomainClient` bakes domain in at construction. Developers can't forget the filter.
5. **Apps are thin.** A page file is 10-20 lines: import shared components, compose, pass brand config. Business logic lives in packages.

---

## Section 2: Composition Manifest and 3-Tier Page System

Each app has a `composition-manifest.ts` — the authoritative record of every route, its tier, indexability, and template config.

### Tier Definitions

| Tier | Description | Indexed? | Override mechanism | Example pages |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1 — Unique** | Brand owns the JSX entirely | Yes | Normal page file, no template | Homepage, About, LRI, Concierge |
| **2 — Configurable** | Same building blocks, different arrangement | Yes | Template + layout/sections config | Search, Building Detail, City, Blog, Tools |
| **3 — Shared** | Identical structure, brand-skinned via tokens | Usually no | One-liner wrapping a template | Privacy, Login, Admin, Favorites |

### Manifest Shape

```ts
export type CompositionTier = 1 | 2 | 3;

export interface ManifestEntry {
  tier: CompositionTier;
  indexed: boolean;
  template?: string;          // tier 2 and 3 only
  layout?: string;            // tier 2 only — controls section arrangement
  sections?: string[];        // tier 2 only — which blocks to render, in order
}

export type CompositionManifest = Record<string, ManifestEntry>;
```

### Example Manifest (Luxury Apartments)

```ts
export const manifest: CompositionManifest = {
  // Tier 1: Unique
  '/':                    { tier: 1, indexed: true },
  '/about':               { tier: 1, indexed: true },
  '/lri':                 { tier: 1, indexed: true },
  '/concierge':           { tier: 1, indexed: true },
  '/for-buildings':       { tier: 1, indexed: true },
  '/contact':             { tier: 1, indexed: true },
  '/best':                { tier: 1, indexed: true },
  '/press':               { tier: 1, indexed: true },

  // Tier 2: Configurable
  '/search':              { tier: 2, indexed: true, template: 'search',
                            layout: 'map-right',
                            sections: ['hero-search', 'filters', 'results-grid', 'map', 'cta-newsletter'] },
  '/buildings':           { tier: 2, indexed: true, template: 'buildings',
                            layout: 'card-grid',
                            sections: ['hero', 'filter-bar', 'building-grid', 'neighborhood-links'] },
  '/buildings/[slug]':    { tier: 2, indexed: true, template: 'building-detail',
                            layout: 'gallery-top',
                            sections: ['gallery', 'overview', 'units', 'amenities', 'neighborhood', 'similar'] },
  '/apartments/[slug]':   { tier: 2, indexed: true, template: 'listing-detail',
                            layout: 'gallery-left',
                            sections: ['gallery', 'pricing', 'features', 'building-link', 'neighborhood', 'similar'] },
  '/cities/[slug]':       { tier: 2, indexed: true, template: 'city',
                            layout: 'hero-stats',
                            sections: ['hero', 'stats', 'neighborhoods', 'buildings', 'guides', 'faq'] },
  '/neighborhoods/[slug]':{ tier: 2, indexed: true, template: 'neighborhood',
                            layout: 'hero-map',
                            sections: ['hero', 'scores', 'map', 'buildings', 'restaurants', 'transit'] },
  '/blog':                { tier: 2, indexed: true, template: 'blog-index',
                            layout: 'featured-top',
                            sections: ['featured', 'recent-grid', 'categories', 'newsletter'] },
  '/blog/[slug]':         { tier: 2, indexed: true, template: 'blog-post',
                            layout: 'article',
                            sections: ['hero-image', 'body', 'author', 'related', 'newsletter'] },
  '/tools/[slug]':        { tier: 2, indexed: true, template: 'tool',
                            layout: 'calculator',
                            sections: ['tool-widget', 'explanation', 'related-tools', 'cta'] },

  // Tier 3: Shared
  '/privacy':             { tier: 3, indexed: false, template: 'legal' },
  '/terms':               { tier: 3, indexed: false, template: 'legal' },
  '/login':               { tier: 3, indexed: false, template: 'auth' },
  '/signup':              { tier: 3, indexed: false, template: 'auth' },
  '/favorites':           { tier: 3, indexed: false, template: 'user-list' },
  '/compare':             { tier: 3, indexed: false, template: 'user-list' },
  '/admin/*':             { tier: 3, indexed: false, template: 'admin' },

```

### How Each Tier Works

**Tier 1 page file:**
```tsx
// apps/luxury-apartments/src/app/about/page.tsx
// Normal JSX — imports from @/lib/ui, owns its own layout
import { PageShell, Hero, TeamGrid } from '@/lib/ui';
export default function AboutPage() { /* brand-specific composition */ }
```

**Tier 2 page file:**
```tsx
// apps/luxury-apartments/src/app/search/page.tsx
import { SearchTemplate } from '@luxurydomains/ui/compositions/SearchTemplate';
import { manifest } from '@/composition-manifest';
import { getDomainClient } from '@/lib/supabase/domain-client';

export default async function SearchPage({ searchParams }) {
  const db = await getDomainClient();
  const config = manifest['/search'];
  return (
    <SearchTemplate
      layout={config.layout}
      sections={config.sections}
      initialData={await db.getApartments({ limit: 24 })}
    />
  );
}
```

**Tier 3 page file:**
```tsx
// apps/luxury-apartments/src/app/privacy/page.tsx
import { LegalTemplate } from '@luxurydomains/ui/layouts/LegalTemplate';
import privacyContent from '@/content/privacy.mdx';
export default function PrivacyPage() {
  return <LegalTemplate>{privacyContent}</LegalTemplate>;
}
```

### Build-Time Manifest Validation

Runs as a root-level Turborepo task before any app builds:

1. Every `app/*/page.tsx` must have a manifest entry
2. Every manifest entry must have a page file
3. Indexed tier-2 routes shared across 2+ apps: **fail build** if identical `layout` + `sections` config
4. Warning (not failure) if tier-2 sections overlap >80%
5. Tier-1 and tier-3 routes: no cross-domain check needed

---

## Section 3: Hybrid Database Schema

### Shared Tables

All apps query these, filtered by `domain_visibility`:

- `apartments`, `buildings`, `neighborhoods`, `cities`, `states`
- `users`, `user_profiles`, `user_favorites`, `user_saved_searches`, `user_collections`
- `tour_sessions`, `tour_feedback`

```sql
ALTER TABLE apartments ADD COLUMN domain_visibility text[]
  DEFAULT ARRAY['luxuryapartments','lofts','d3','d4'];
CREATE INDEX idx_apartments_domain ON apartments USING GIN (domain_visibility);
```

### Per-Domain Tables

Prefixed by domain abbreviation, queried from app-local `src/lib/data/`:

- `la_blog_posts`, `ll_blog_posts`, `d3_blog_posts`, `d4_blog_posts`
- `la_leads`, `ll_leads`, etc.
- `la_form_submissions`, `ll_form_submissions`, etc.
- `la_editorial_content`, `ll_editorial_content`, etc.

### RLS Enforcement

Safety net for anon/authenticated queries:

```sql
CREATE POLICY "domain_filter" ON apartments FOR SELECT
USING (
  domain_visibility @> ARRAY[current_setting('app.current_domain', true)]
);
ALTER TABLE apartments FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
```

The `createDomainClient` factory sets the session variable automatically:

```ts
export async function createDomainClient(supabase: SupabaseClient, domain: BrandId) {
  await supabase.rpc('set_domain_context', { domain });
  return {
    getApartments: (filters?) => queryApartments(supabase, domain, filters),
    getNeighborhoods: () => queryNeighborhoods(supabase, domain),
    // ...
  };
}
```

**Note:** Service role key bypasses RLS. Server-side ISR using service role relies on the factory's application-layer domain filter, not RLS.

### Revalidation

When `domain_visibility` changes, a Supabase Edge Function webhooks all apps:

```ts
for (const [domain, url] of Object.entries(APP_URLS)) {
  await fetch(`${url}/api/revalidate`, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${REVALIDATE_SECRET}` },
    body: JSON.stringify({ tag: `apartment-${record.id}` }),
  });
}
```

Tag-based, not path-based — slugs may differ by domain.

---

## Section 4: Brand Override System

### Barrel File Re-Export (one per app)

```ts
// apps/lofts/src/lib/ui.ts
export * from '@luxurydomains/ui';           // everything from shared
export { Hero } from '../components/Hero';    // local override shadows the wildcard
export { Footer } from '../components/Footer';
```

### ESLint Enforcement

```json
{
  "rules": {
    "no-restricted-imports": ["error", {
      "paths": [{
        "name": "@luxurydomains/ui",
        "message": "Import from @/lib/ui instead, so brand overrides are applied."
      }]
    }]
  }
}
```

### Sub-Component Composition (Harry's Forge Pattern)

Shared components export primitives for brand assembly:

```tsx
// packages/ui/compositions/Hero.tsx
export function HeroLayout({ children, variant = 'split' }) { /* ... */ }
export function HeroImage({ src, overlay = false }) { /* ... */ }
export function HeroHeadline({ children, size = 'lg' }) { /* ... */ }
export function HeroCTA({ children, href }) { /* ... */ }

// Default composition
export function Hero({ title, image, cta }) {
  return (
    <HeroLayout variant="split">
      <HeroHeadline size="lg">{title}</HeroHeadline>
      <HeroCTA href={cta.href}>{cta.label}</HeroCTA>
      <HeroImage src={image} />
    </HeroLayout>
  );
}
```

Brand override assembles the same primitives differently:

```tsx
// apps/lofts/src/components/Hero.tsx
import { HeroLayout, HeroImage, HeroHeadline, HeroCTA } from '@luxurydomains/ui';

export function Hero({ title, image, cta }) {
  return (
    <HeroLayout variant="fullbleed">
      <HeroImage src={image} overlay />
      <HeroHeadline size="xl">{title}</HeroHeadline>
      <HeroCTA href={cta.href}>{cta.label}</HeroCTA>
    </HeroLayout>
  );
}
```

### Rules

- Overrides must satisfy the same TypeScript interface as the shared component
- Primitives (Button, Input, Badge) should almost never be overridden — use tokens instead
- Compositions (Hero, Footer, SearchBar) are the expected override targets
- If an override needs a new prop, add it to the shared interface with a default value

---

## Section 5: Design Token System

### Token Schema (two tiers)

```ts
interface RequiredBrandTokens {
  colors: {
    primary: string; primaryHover: string; primaryForeground: string;
    secondary: string; secondaryHover: string; secondaryForeground: string;
    accent: string; accentForeground: string;
    surface: string; surfaceElevated: string;
    border: string;
    text: string; textMuted: string; textInverse: string;
    success: string; warning: string; error: string;
    overlay: string;
  };
  fonts: { display: string; body: string; mono: string; };
  radii: { card: string; button: string; input: string; };
}

interface OptionalBrandTokens {
  colors: { surfaceSunken: string; borderStrong: string; textDisabled: string; };
  shadows: { card: string; overlay: string; xs: string; sm: string; md: string; lg: string; xl: string; };
  motion: { durationFast: string; durationNormal: string; durationSlow: string;
            easeDefault: string; easeBrand: string; };
  image: { radius: string; overlayOpacity: string; aspectCard: string; aspectHero: string; };
  spacing: { section: string; container: string; };
  tracking: { tight: string; normal: string; wide: string; };
  darkMode: boolean;
}

type BrandTokens = RequiredBrandTokens & Partial<OptionalBrandTokens>;
```

### Flow: TS → CSS → Tailwind

1. Brand author writes `brand.config.ts` using `satisfies BrandTokens`
2. `generate.ts` script reads the TS config + merges with `defaults.ts` for optional tokens
3. Outputs a CSS file with `@theme { }` block
4. App's `globals.css` imports: `@import "@luxurydomains/design-tokens/luxury-apartments.css"`
5. Tailwind v4 auto-generates utilities: `bg-primary`, `font-display`, `rounded-card`, `shadow-card`, etc.

### Validation

- **Compile time:** TypeScript `satisfies` catches missing required tokens
- **CI time:** Zod schema validates value formats (e.g., color values match `oklch|#|rgb` pattern)
- **Build time:** `generate.ts` fails if required fields resolve to undefined after merge

### Dark Mode

Per-brand opt-in via `data-theme` attribute:

```css
[data-theme="dark"] {
  --color-surface: oklch(0.12 0.02 260);
  --color-text: oklch(0.95 0.01 260);
}
```

Brands without dark mode omit these blocks entirely. Layout component checks `brand.darkMode` before rendering the toggle.

## Section 6: Deployment and CI/CD

### Vercel Configuration

| Project | Root Directory | Domain |
|---|---|---|
| luxury-apartments | `apps/luxury-apartments` | luxuryapartments.com |
| lofts | `apps/lofts` | lofts.com |
| d3 | `apps/d3` | TBD |

### Turborepo Pipeline

```json
{
  "tasks": {
    "generate-tokens": {
      "inputs": ["packages/design-tokens/src/**"],
      "outputs": ["packages/design-tokens/generated/**"]
    },
    "validate-manifests": {
      "inputs": ["apps/*/src/composition-manifest.ts"]
    },
    "validate-brand": {
      "dependsOn": ["generate-tokens"],
      "inputs": ["brand.config.ts"]
    },
    "build": {
      "dependsOn": ["generate-tokens", "validate-brand", "validate-manifests"],
      "outputs": [".next/**"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Per-Domain Services

- Google Analytics: 4 separate GA4 properties
- Google Search Console: 4 separate properties
- PostHog: 4 separate projects
- Sentry: 1 project with domain tags
- Supabase: 1 project (shared database)

## Section 7: Migration Path

### Phase 0: Launch Lofts (now)
- Port missing features from LA manually
- Launch on standalone repo
- Acceptable tech debt — eliminated in Phase 3

### Phase 1: Scaffold Monorepo (when D3 planning begins)
- Create `C:\Dev\LuxuryDomains\` with Turborepo + pnpm
- Extract `packages/shared/` (pure utilities)
- Extract `packages/design-tokens/` (schema + LA tokens + generation)
- Extract `packages/data/` (Supabase types + shared queries + factory)
- Move LA into `apps/luxury-apartments/`
- Gate: LA builds identically from monorepo

### Phase 2: Extract UI Package
- Move components to `packages/ui/` (primitives + compositions)
- Create barrel file (initially zero overrides — all re-exports)
- Set up ESLint rule
- Gate: LA builds identically

### Phase 3: Port Lofts
- Move Lofts into `apps/lofts/`
- Replace local copies with package imports
- Create Lofts override barrel + brand tokens + composition manifest
- Gate: Lofts builds from monorepo

### Phase 4: Extract SEO + Tooling
- Move SEO utilities to `packages/seo/`
- Build manifest validator
- Build `/add-page` skill integration
- Wire cross-domain build validation

### Phase 5: D3 Onboarding
- New app in `apps/d3/`
- First real test with a fresh domain
- Starts from shared packages, writes brand config + manifest + overrides
